entities.name: android
entities.brief: The Android platform on which the Android application is running.

entities.name: android.app.state
entities.brief: This attribute represents the state of the application.
entities.note: The Android lifecycle states are defined in [Activity lifecycle callbacks](https://developer.android.com/guide/components/activities/activity-lifecycle#lifecycle-callbacks), and from which the `OS identifiers` are derived.

entities.name: android.os.api_level
entities.brief: Uniquely identifies the framework API revision offered by a version (`os.version`) of the android operating system. More information can be found in the [Android API levels documentation](https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/manifest/uses-sdk-element#ApiLevels).

entities.name: android.state
entities.brief: Deprecated. Use `android.app.state` attribute instead.

entities.name: anthropic.inference.client
entities.brief: Semantic Conventions for [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/) client spans extend and override the semantic conventions for [Gen AI Spans](gen-ai-spans.md).
entities.note:
`gen_ai.provider.name` MUST be set to `"anthropic"` and SHOULD be provided **at span creation time**.

**Span name** SHOULD be `{gen_ai.operation.name} {gen_ai.request.model}`.

deprecation.note: Moved to the [OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions repository](https://github.com/open-telemetry/semantic-conventions-genai).

entities.name: app
entities.brief: An app used directly by end users — like mobile, web, or desktop.

entities.name: app.build_id
entities.brief: Unique identifier for a particular build or compilation of the application.

entities.name: app.crash
entities.brief:
This event represents a termination of a user-facing application due to
unrecoverable programming errors such as exceptions or signals that
indicate an error has happened at a lower level.
entities.note:
Crash events can be produced asynchronously by an OTel SDK instance that is
not running in the application instance in which the crash happened. For
example, the instrumentation may report crashes from previous app instances
based on information found in tombstones on disk.
If the reporter of the crash is not the crashing application instance itself,
relevant resource attributes that identify the application instance that
crashed MUST be provided as event attributes so that the corresponding
attributes from the reporter's resource aren't used instead.
How the instrumentation will determine whether an instance of a crash has
already been reported and how the necessary data will be retrieved is left up
to the instrumentation. Providing enough data to dedupe is NOT REQUIRED.

entities.name: app.crash.id
entities.brief: A unique identifier representing an instance of an end-user facing app crash.
entities.note:
Its value MAY be meaningful and be used as a reference for telemetry and metadata recorded by
the same instrumentation (e.g. it is an ID generated by an external source that captured the crash).
It MAY come from a source external to the instrumentation such that it can be used to look up additional
data from other sources as well as facilitate deduplication.

entities.name: app.installation.id
entities.brief: A unique identifier representing the installation of an application on a specific device
entities.note:
Its value SHOULD persist across launches of the same application installation, including through application upgrades.
It SHOULD change if the application is uninstalled or if all applications of the vendor are uninstalled.
Additionally, users might be able to reset this value (e.g. by clearing application data).
If an app is installed multiple times on the same device (e.g. in different accounts on Android), each `app.installation.id` SHOULD have a different value.
If multiple OpenTelemetry SDKs are used within the same application, they SHOULD use the same value for `app.installation.id`.
Hardware IDs (e.g. serial number, IMEI, MAC address) MUST NOT be used as the `app.installation.id`.

For iOS, this value SHOULD be equal to the [vendor identifier](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uidevice/identifierforvendor).

For Android, examples of `app.installation.id` implementations include:

- [Firebase Installation ID](https://firebase.google.com/docs/projects/manage-installations).
- A globally unique UUID which is persisted across sessions in your application.
- [App set ID](https://developer.android.com/identity/app-set-id).
- [`Settings.getString(Settings.Secure.ANDROID_ID)`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/provider/Settings.Secure#ANDROID_ID).

More information about Android identifier best practices can be found in the [Android user data IDs guide](https://developer.android.com/training/articles/user-data-ids).

entities.name: app.jank
entities.brief: This event indicates that the application has detected substandard UI rendering performance.
entities.note: Jank happens when the UI is rendered slowly enough for the user to experience some disruption or sluggishness.

entities.name: app.jank.frame_count
entities.brief: A number of frame renders that experienced jank.
entities.note: Depending on platform limitations, the value provided MAY be approximation.

entities.name: app.jank.period
entities.brief: The time period, in seconds, for which this jank is being reported.

entities.name: app.jank.threshold
entities.brief: The minimum rendering threshold for this jank, in seconds.

entities.name: app.screen.click
entities.brief: This event represents an instantaneous click on the screen of an application.
entities.note: The `app.screen.click` event can be used to indicate that a user has clicked or tapped on the screen portion of an application. Clicks outside of an application's active area SHOULD NOT generate this event. This event does not differentiate between touch/mouse down and touch/mouse up. Implementations SHOULD give preference to generating this event at the time the click is complete, typically on touch release or mouse up. The location of the click event MUST be provided in absolute screen pixels.

entities.name: app.screen.coordinate.x
entities.brief: The x (horizontal) coordinate of a screen coordinate, in screen pixels.

entities.name: app.screen.coordinate.y
entities.brief: The y (vertical) component of a screen coordinate, in screen pixels.

entities.name: app.screen.id
entities.brief: An identifier that uniquely differentiates this screen from other screens in the same application.
entities.note: A screen represents only the part of the device display drawn by the app. It typically contains multiple widgets or UI components and is larger in scope than individual widgets. Multiple screens can coexist on the same display simultaneously (e.g., split view on tablets).

entities.name: app.screen.name
entities.brief: The name of an application screen.
entities.note: A screen represents only the part of the device display drawn by the app. It typically contains multiple widgets or UI components and is larger in scope than individual widgets. Multiple screens can coexist on the same display simultaneously (e.g., split view on tablets).

entities.name: app.widget.click
entities.brief: This event indicates that an application widget has been clicked.
entities.note: Use this event to indicate that visual application component has been clicked, typically through a user's manual interaction.

entities.name: app.widget.id
entities.brief: An identifier that uniquely differentiates this widget from other widgets in the same application.
entities.note: A widget is an application component, typically an on-screen visual GUI element.

entities.name: app.widget.name
entities.brief: The name of an application widget.
entities.note: A widget is an application component, typically an on-screen visual GUI element.

entities.name: artifact.attestation.filename
entities.brief: The provenance filename of the built attestation which directly relates to the build artifact filename. This filename SHOULD accompany the artifact at publish time. See the [SLSA Relationship](https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.0/distributing-provenance#relationship-between-artifacts-and-attestations) specification for more information.

entities.name: artifact.attestation.hash
entities.brief: The full [hash value (see glossary)](https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/FIPS/NIST.FIPS.186-5.pdf), of the built attestation. Some envelopes in the [software attestation space](https://github.com/in-toto/attestation/tree/main/spec) also refer to this as the **digest**.

entities.name: artifact.attestation.id
entities.brief: The id of the build [software attestation](https://slsa.dev/attestation-model).

entities.name: artifact.filename
entities.brief: The human readable file name of the artifact, typically generated during build and release processes. Often includes the package name and version in the file name.
entities.note:
This file name can also act as the [Package Name](https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.0/terminology#package-model)
in cases where the package ecosystem maps accordingly.
Additionally, the artifact [can be published](https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.0/terminology#software-supply-chain)
for others, but that is not a guarantee.

entities.name: artifact.hash
entities.brief: The full [hash value (see glossary)](https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/FIPS/NIST.FIPS.186-5.pdf), often found in checksum.txt on a release of the artifact and used to verify package integrity.
entities.note:
The specific algorithm used to create the cryptographic hash value is
not defined. In situations where an artifact has multiple
cryptographic hashes, it is up to the implementer to choose which
hash value to set here; this should be the most secure hash algorithm
that is suitable for the situation and consistent with the
corresponding attestation. The implementer can then provide the other
hash values through an additional set of attribute extensions as they
deem necessary.

entities.name: artifact.purl
entities.brief: The [Package URL](https://github.com/package-url/purl-spec) of the [package artifact](https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.0/terminology#package-model) provides a standard way to identify and locate the packaged artifact.

entities.name: artifact.version
entities.brief: The version of the artifact.

entities.name: aspnetcore.authentication.challenges

entities.name: aspnetcore.diagnostics.exceptions

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.error_code

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.password_check_result
entities.brief: The result from checking the password.

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.result
entities.brief: The result of the identity operation.

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.sign_in.authenticate.duration
entities.brief: The duration of authenticate attempts. The authenticate metrics is recorded by sign in methods such as PasswordSignInAsync and TwoFactorSignInAsync.
entities.note: Meter name: `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Identity`; Added in: ASP.NET Core 10.0

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.sign_in.check_password_attempts
entities.brief: The total number of check password attempts. Checks that the account is in a state that can log in and that the password is valid using the UserManager.CheckPasswordAsync method.

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.sign_in.sign_ins

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.sign_in.two_factor_clients_forgotten

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.sign_in.type
entities.brief: The authentication type.

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.token_purpose

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.token_verified
entities.brief: The result of token verification.

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.user_type
entities.brief: The full name of the identity user type.

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.user.check_password_attempts
entities.brief: The number of check password attempts. Only checks whether the password is valid and not whether the user account is in a state that can log in.

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.user.create.duration
entities.brief: The duration of user creation operations.

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.user.delete.duration
entities.brief: The duration of user deletion operations.
entities.note: Meter name: `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Identity`; Added in: ASP.NET Core 10.0

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.user.generated_tokens
entities.brief: The total number of token generations.
entities.note: Meter name: `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Identity`; Added in: ASP.NET Core 10.0

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.user.update_type
entities.brief: The user update type.

entities.name: aspnetcore.identity.user.update.duration
entities.brief: The duration of user update operations.

entities.name: aspnetcore.memory_pool.allocated
entities.brief: Total number of bytes allocated by the memory pool. Allocation occurs when a memory rental request exceeds the available pooled memory.
entities.note: Meter name: `Microsoft.AspNetCore.MemoryPool`; Added in: ASP.NET Core 10.0

entities.name: aspnetcore.memory_pool.evicted

entities.name: aspnetcore.memory_pool.pooled

entities.name: aspnetcore.memory_pool.rented

entities.name: aspnetcore.rate_limiting.active_request_leases
entities.brief: Number of requests that are currently active on the server that hold a rate limiting lease.
entities.note: Meter name: `Microsoft.AspNetCore.RateLimiting`; Added in: ASP.NET Core 8.0

entities.name: aspnetcore.rate_limiting.policy

entities.name: aspnetcore.rate_limiting.queued_requests

entities.name: aspnetcore.rate_limiting.request_lease.duration

entities.name: aws
entities.brief:
The `aws` conventions apply to operations using the AWS SDK. They map request or response parameters in AWS SDK API calls to attributes on a Span. The conventions have been collected over time based on feedback from AWS users of tracing and will continue to evolve as new interesting conventions are found.
Some descriptions are also provided for populating general OpenTelemetry semantic conventions based on these APIs.

entities.name: aws.bedrock.client
entities.brief: Describes an AWS Bedrock operation span.

deprecation.note: Moved to the [OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions repository](https://github.com/open-telemetry/semantic-conventions-genai).

entities.name: aws.bedrock.guardrail.id
entities.brief: The unique identifier of the AWS Bedrock Guardrail. A [guardrail](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/guardrails.html) helps safeguard and prevent unwanted behavior from model responses or user messages.

entities.name: aws.bedrock.knowledge_base.id
entities.brief: The unique identifier of the AWS Bedrock Knowledge base. A [knowledge base](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/knowledge-base.html) is a bank of information that can be queried by models to generate more relevant responses and augment prompts.

entities.name: aws.client
entities.brief: This span describes an AWS SDK client call.
entities.note:
**Span name** MUST be of the format `Service.Operation` as per the
AWS HTTP API, e.g., `DynamoDB.GetItem`, `S3.ListBuckets`. This is
equivalent to concatenating `rpc.service` and `rpc.method` with `.` and
consistent with the naming guidelines for RPC client spans.

AWS SDK span attributes are based on the request or response parameters
in AWS SDK API calls. The conventions have been collected over time based
on feedback from AWS users of tracing and will continue to increase as new
interesting conventions are found.

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.attribute_definitions
entities.brief: The JSON-serialized value of each item in the `AttributeDefinitions` request field.

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.attributes_to_get
entities.brief: The value of the `AttributesToGet` request parameter.

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.consistent_read
entities.brief: The value of the `ConsistentRead` request parameter.

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.consumed_capacity
entities.brief: The JSON-serialized value of each item in the `ConsumedCapacity` response field.
examples: { "CapacityUnits": number, "GlobalSecondaryIndexes": { "string" : { "CapacityUnits": number, "ReadCapacityUnits": number, "WriteCapacityUnits": number } }, "LocalSecondaryIndexes": { "string" : { "CapacityUnits": number, "ReadCapacityUnits": number, "WriteCapacityUnits": number } }, "ReadCapacityUnits": number, "Table": { "CapacityUnits": number, "ReadCapacityUnits": number, "WriteCapacityUnits": number }, "TableName": "string", "WriteCapacityUnits": number }

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.count

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.exclusive_start_table

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.global_secondary_index_updates
entities.brief: The JSON-serialized value of each item in the `GlobalSecondaryIndexUpdates` request field.

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.global_secondary_indexes
entities.brief: The JSON-serialized value of each item of the `GlobalSecondaryIndexes` request field

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.index_name
entities.brief: The value of the `IndexName` request parameter.

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.item_collection_metrics

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.limit
entities.brief: The value of the `Limit` request parameter.

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.local_secondary_indexes
entities.brief: The JSON-serialized value of each item of the `LocalSecondaryIndexes` request field.

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.projection

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.provisioned_read_capacity

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.provisioned_write_capacity

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.scan_forward

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.scanned_count

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.segment

entities.name: aws.dynamodb.select

entities.name: aws.ecs
entities.brief: Entities used by AWS Elastic Container Service (ECS).

entities.name: aws.ecs.container.arn

entities.name: aws.ecs.task.arn

entities.name: aws.ecs.task.family

entities.name: aws.ecs.task.revision

entities.name: aws.eks

entities.name: aws.eks.cluster.arn

entities.name: aws.extended_request_id
entities.brief: The AWS extended request ID as returned in the response header `x-amz-id-2`.

entities.name: aws.kinesis.stream_name
entities.brief: The name of the AWS Kinesis [stream](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/streams/latest/dev/introduction.html) the request refers to. Corresponds to the `--stream-name` parameter of the Kinesis [describe-stream](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/kinesis/describe-stream.html) operation.

entities.name: aws.lambda
entities.brief: Span attributes used by AWS Lambda (in addition to general `faas` attributes).

entities.name: aws.lambda.invoked_arn
entities.brief: The full invoked ARN as provided on the `Context` passed to the function (`Lambda-Runtime-Invoked-Function-Arn` header on the `/runtime/invocation/next` applicable).
entities.note: This may be different from `cloud.resource_id` if an alias is involved.

entities.name: aws.lambda.resource_mapping.id
entities.brief: The UUID of the [AWS Lambda EvenSource Mapping](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-lambda-eventsourcemapping.html). An event source is mapped to a lambda function. It's contents are read by Lambda and used to trigger a function. This isn't available in the lambda execution context or the lambda runtime environment. This is going to be populated by the AWS SDK for each language when that UUID is present. Some of these operations are Create/Delete/Get/List/Update EventSourceMapping.

entities.name: aws.lambda.server
entities.brief: This span represents AWS Lambda invocation.
entities.note:
Consider setting other attributes of the [`faas` resource][faasres] and [trace][faas] conventions
and the [cloud resource conventions][cloud].

**Span name** MUST be set to the function name from the Lambda `Context`
unless stated otherwise.

**Span kind** MUST be `SERVER` unless stated otherwise.

entities.name: aws.log
entities.brief: Entities specific to Amazon Web Services.

entities.name: aws.log.group.arns
entities.brief: The Amazon Resource Name(s) (ARN) of the AWS log group(s).
entities.note: See the [log group ARN format documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/iam-access-control-overview-cwl.html#CWL_ARN_Format).

entities.name: aws.log.group.names
entities.brief: The name(s) of the AWS log group(s) an application is writing to.
entities.note: Multiple log groups must be supported for cases like multi-container applications, where a single application has sidecar containers, and each write to their own log group.

entities.name: aws.log.stream.arns
entities.brief: The ARN(s) of the AWS log stream(s).
entities.note: See the [log stream ARN format documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/iam-access-control-overview-cwl.html#CWL_ARN_Format). One log group can contain several log streams, so these ARNs necessarily identify both a log group and a log stream.

entities.name: aws.log.stream.names
entities.brief: The name(s) of the AWS log stream(s) an application is writing to.

entities.name: aws.request_id
entities.brief: The AWS request ID as returned in the response headers `x-amzn-requestid`, `x-amzn-request-id` or `x-amz-request-id`.

entities.name: aws.s3
entities.brief: Attributes that exist for S3 request types.

entities.name: aws.s3.bucket
entities.brief: The S3 bucket name the request refers to. Corresponds to the `--bucket` parameter of the [S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/index.html) operations.
entities.note:
The `bucket` attribute is applicable to all S3 operations that reference a bucket, i.e. that require the bucket name as a mandatory parameter.
This applies to almost all S3 operations except `list-buckets`.

entities.name: aws.s3.client
entities.brief: Semantic Conventions for AWS S3 client spans extend the general [AWS SDK Semantic Conventions](/docs/cloud-providers/aws-sdk.md)

entities.name: aws.s3.copy_source
entities.brief: The source object (in the form `bucket`/`key`) for the copy operation.
entities.note:
The `copy_source` attribute applies to S3 copy operations and corresponds to the `--copy-source` parameter
of the [copy-object operation within the S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/copy-object.html).
This applies in particular to the following operations:

- [copy-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/copy-object.html)
- [upload-part-copy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part-copy.html)

entities.name: aws.s3.delete
entities.brief: The delete request container that specifies the objects to be deleted.
entities.note:
The `delete` attribute is only applicable to the [delete-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/delete-object.html) operation.
The `delete` attribute corresponds to the `--delete` parameter of the
[delete-objects operation within the S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/delete-objects.html).

entities.name: aws.s3.key
entities.brief: The S3 object key the request refers to. Corresponds to the `--key` parameter of the [S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/index.html) operations.
entities.note:
The `key` attribute is applicable to all object-related S3 operations, i.e. that require the object key as a mandatory parameter.
This applies in particular to the following operations:

- [copy-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/copy-object.html)
- [delete-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/delete-object.html)
- [get-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/get-object.html)
- [head-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/head-object.html)
- [put-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/put-object.html)
- [restore-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/restore-object.html)
- [select-object-content](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/select-object-content.html)
- [abort-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/abort-multipart-upload.html)
- [complete-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/complete-multipart-upload.html)
- [create-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/create-multipart-upload.html)
- [list-parts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/list-parts.html)
- [upload-part](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part.html)
- [upload-part-copy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part-copy.html)

entities.name: aws.s3.part_number
entities.brief: The part number of the part being uploaded in a multipart-upload operation. This is a positive integer between 1 and 10,000.
entities.note:
The `part_number` attribute is only applicable to the [upload-part](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part.html)
and [upload-part-copy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part-copy.html) operations.
The `part_number` attribute corresponds to the `--part-number` parameter of the
[upload-part operation within the S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part.html).

entities.name: aws.s3.upload_id
entities.brief: Upload ID that identifies the multipart upload.
entities.note:
The `upload_id` attribute applies to S3 multipart-upload operations and corresponds to the `--upload-id` parameter
of the [S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/index.html) multipart operations.
This applies in particular to the following operations:

- [abort-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/abort-multipart-upload.html)
- [complete-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/complete-multipart-upload.html)
- [list-parts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/list-parts.html)
- [upload-part](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part.html)
- [upload-part-copy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part-copy.html)

entities.name: aws.secretsmanager.secret.arn
entities.brief: The ARN of the Secret stored in the Secrets Manager

entities.name: aws.sns.topic.arn
entities.brief: The ARN of the AWS SNS Topic. An Amazon SNS [topic](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/sns-create-topic.html) is a logical access point that acts as a communication channel.

entities.name: aws.sqs.queue.url
entities.brief: The URL of the AWS SQS Queue. It's a unique identifier for a queue in Amazon Simple Queue Service (SQS) and is used to access the queue and perform actions on it.

entities.name: aws.step_functions.activity.arn
entities.brief: The ARN of the AWS Step Functions Activity.

entities.name: aws.step_functions.state_machine.arn
entities.brief: The ARN of the AWS Step Functions State Machine.

entities.name: az.namespace
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `azure.resource_provider.namespace` instead.

entities.name: az.resource.log
entities.brief: Deprecated. Use `azure.resource.log` instead.

entities.name: az.service_request_id
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `azure.service.request.id` instead.

entities.name: azure.ai.inference.client
entities.brief: Semantic Conventions for [Azure AI Inference](https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/aifoundry/modelinference/) client spans extend and override the semantic conventions for [Gen AI Spans](gen-ai-spans.md).
entities.note:
`gen_ai.provider.name` MUST be set to `"azure.ai.inference"` and SHOULD be provided **at span creation time**.

**Span name** SHOULD be `{gen_ai.operation.name} {gen_ai.request.model}` when the
model name is available and `{gen_ai.operation.name}` otherwise.

deprecation.note: Moved to the [OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions repository](https://github.com/open-telemetry/semantic-conventions-genai).

entities.name: azure.client.id
entities.brief: The unique identifier of the client instance.

entities.name: azure.cosmosdb.client
entities.brief: Cosmos DB instrumentations include call-level spans that represent logical database calls and adhere to the general [Semantic Conventions for Database Client Spans](/docs/db/database-spans.md).
entities.note:
Additional spans representing network calls may also be created depending on the connection mode (Gateway or Direct).
Semantic conventions described in this document apply to the call-level spans only.

`db.system.name` MUST be set to `"azure.cosmosdb"` and SHOULD be provided **at span creation time**.

**Span name** SHOULD follow the general [database span name convention](/docs/db/database-spans.md#name)

entities.name: azure.cosmosdb.client.active_instance.count
entities.brief: Number of active client instances.

entities.name: azure.cosmosdb.client.operation.request_charge
entities.brief: [Request units](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cosmos-db/request-units) consumed by the operation.

entities.name: azure.cosmosdb.connection.mode
entities.brief: Cosmos client connection mode.

entities.name: azure.cosmosdb.consistency.level
entities.brief: Account or request [consistency level](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cosmos-db/consistency-levels).

entities.name: azure.cosmosdb.operation.contacted_regions
entities.brief: List of regions contacted during operation in the order that they were contacted. If there is more than one region listed, it indicates that the operation was performed on multiple regions i.e. cross-regional call.
entities.note: Region name matches the format of `displayName` in [Azure Location API](https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/resources/subscriptions/list-locations)

entities.name: azure.cosmosdb.operation.request_charge
entities.brief: The number of request units consumed by the operation.

entities.name: azure.cosmosdb.request.body.size
entities.brief: Request payload size in bytes.

entities.name: azure.cosmosdb.response.sub_status_code
entities.brief: Cosmos DB sub status code.

entities.name: azure.resource_group.name
entities.brief: The name of the Azure [resource group](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/manage-resource-groups-portal) the resource belongs to.

entities.name: azure.resource_provider.namespace
entities.brief: [Azure Resource Provider Namespace](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/azure-services-resource-providers) as recognized by the client.

entities.name: azure.resource.log
entities.brief: Describes Azure Resource Log event, see [Azure Resource Log Top-level Schema](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/essentials/resource-logs-schema#top-level-common-schema) for more details.

entities.name: azure.service.request.id
entities.brief: The unique identifier of the service request. It's generated by the Azure service and returned with the response.

entities.name: browser
entities.brief: The web browser in which the application represented by the resource is running. The `browser.*` attributes MUST be used only for resources that represent applications running in a web browser (regardless of whether running on a mobile or desktop device).

entities.name: browser.brands
entities.brief: Array of brand name and version separated by a space
entities.note: This value is intended to be taken from the [UA client hints API](https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints/#interface) (`navigator.userAgentData.brands`).

entities.name: browser.document
entities.brief: The web page document loaded in the browser.

entities.name: browser.document.url.full
entities.brief: Absolute URL of the current browser document according to [RFC3986](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986).

entities.name: browser.language
entities.brief: Preferred language of the user using the browser
entities.note: This value is intended to be taken from the Navigator API `navigator.language`.

entities.name: browser.mobile
entities.brief: A boolean that is true if the browser is running on a mobile device
entities.note: This value is intended to be taken from the [UA client hints API](https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints/#interface) (`navigator.userAgentData.mobile`). If unavailable, this attribute SHOULD be left unset.

entities.name: browser.platform
entities.brief: The platform on which the browser is running
entities.note:
This value is intended to be taken from the [UA client hints API](https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints/#interface) (`navigator.userAgentData.platform`). If unavailable, the legacy `navigator.platform` API SHOULD NOT be used instead and this attribute SHOULD be left unset in order for the values to be consistent.
The list of possible values is defined in the [W3C User-Agent Client Hints specification](https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints/#sec-ch-ua-platform). Note that some (but not all) of these values can overlap with values in the [`os.type` and `os.name` attributes](./os.md). However, for consistency, the values in the `browser.platform` attribute should capture the exact value that the user agent provides.

entities.name: browser.web_vital
entities.brief: This event describes the website performance metrics introduced by Google, See [web vitals](https://web.dev/vitals).

entities.name: cassandra.consistency.level
entities.brief: The consistency level of the query. Based on consistency values from [CQL](https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/dml/dmlConfigConsistency.html).

entities.name: cassandra.coordinator.dc
entities.brief: The data center of the coordinating node for a query.

entities.name: cassandra.coordinator.id
entities.brief: The ID of the coordinating node for a query.

entities.name: cassandra.page.size
entities.brief: The fetch size used for paging, i.e. how many rows will be returned at once.

entities.name: cassandra.query.idempotent
entities.brief: Whether or not the query is idempotent.

entities.name: cassandra.speculative_execution.count
entities.brief: The number of times a query was speculatively executed. Not set or `0` if the query was not executed speculatively.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline
entities.brief: A pipeline is a series of automated steps that helps software teams deliver code.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.action.name
entities.brief: The kind of action a pipeline run is performing.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.name
entities.brief: The human readable name of the pipeline within a CI/CD system.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.result
entities.brief: The result of a pipeline run.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.run
entities.brief: A pipeline run is a singular execution of a given pipeline's tasks.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.run.active
entities.brief: The number of pipeline runs currently active in the system by state.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.run.duration
entities.brief: Duration of a pipeline run grouped by pipeline, state and result.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.run.errors
entities.brief: The number of errors encountered in pipeline runs (eg. compile, test failures).
entities.note:
There might be errors in a pipeline run that are non fatal (eg. they are suppressed) or in a parallel stage multiple stages could have a fatal error.
This means that this error count might not be the same as the count of metric `cicd.pipeline.run.duration` with run result `failure`.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.run.id
entities.brief: The unique identifier of a pipeline run within a CI/CD system.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.run.server
entities.brief: This span describes a CI/CD pipeline run.
entities.note:
For all pipeline runs, a span with kind `SERVER` SHOULD be created corresponding to the execution of the pipeline run.

**Span name** MUST follow the overall [guidelines for span names](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/v1.58.0/specification/trace/api.md#span).

The span name SHOULD be `{action} {pipeline}` if there is a (low-cardinality) pipeline name available.
If the pipeline name is not available or is likely to have high cardinality, then the span name SHOULD be `{action}`.

The `{action}` SHOULD be the [`cicd.pipeline.action.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/cicd.md#cicd-pipeline-action-name).

The `{pipeline}` SHOULD be the [`cicd.pipeline.name`](/docs/registry/attributes/cicd.md#cicd-pipeline-name).

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.run.state
entities.brief: The pipeline run goes through these states during its lifecycle.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.run.url.full
entities.brief: The [URL](https://wikipedia.org/wiki/URL) of the pipeline run, providing the complete address in order to locate and identify the pipeline run.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.task.internal
entities.brief: This span describes task execution in a pipeline run.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.task.name
entities.brief: The human readable name of a task within a pipeline. Task here most closely aligns with a [computing process](https://wikipedia.org/wiki/Pipeline_(computing)) in a pipeline. Other terms for tasks include commands, steps, and procedures.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.task.run.id
entities.brief: The unique identifier of a task run within a pipeline.
entities.note: For a given pipeline run and task, the `cicd.pipeline.task.run.id` MUST be unique within that run. For the same task across different runs of the same pipeline, the `cicd.pipeline.task.run.id` MAY remain the same, enabling correlation of `cicd.pipeline.task.run.result` values across multiple pipeline runs.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.task.run.result
entities.brief: The result of a task run.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.task.run.url.full
entities.brief: The [URL](https://wikipedia.org/wiki/URL) of the pipeline task run, providing the complete address in order to locate and identify the pipeline task run.

entities.name: cicd.pipeline.task.type
entities.brief: The type of the task within a pipeline.

entities.name: cicd.system.component
entities.brief: The name of a component of the CI/CD system.

entities.name: cicd.system.errors
entities.brief: The number of errors in a component of the CI/CD system (eg. controller, scheduler, agent).
entities.note: Errors in pipeline run execution are explicitly excluded. Ie a test failure is not counted in this metric.

entities.name: cicd.worker
entities.brief:
A CI/CD worker is a component of the CI/CD system that performs work (eg. running pipeline tasks or performing sync).
A single pipeline run may be distributed across multiple workers. Any OpenTelemetry signal associated with a worker should be associated to the worker that performed the corresponding work.
For example, when a pipeline run involves several workers, its task run spans may reference the different `cicd.worker` resources corresponding to the workers that executed each task run. The pipeline run's parent span may instead reference the CI/CD controller as the `cicd.worker` resource.

entities.name: cicd.worker.count
entities.brief: The number of workers on the CI/CD system by state.

entities.name: cicd.worker.id
entities.brief: The unique identifier of a worker within a CI/CD system.

entities.name: cicd.worker.name
entities.brief: The name of a worker within a CI/CD system.

entities.name: cicd.worker.state
entities.brief: The state of a CI/CD worker / agent.

entities.name: cicd.worker.url.full
entities.brief: The [URL](https://wikipedia.org/wiki/URL) of the worker, providing the complete address in order to locate and identify the worker.

entities.name: cli.client
entities.brief: This span describes CLI (Command Line Interfaces) program execution from a caller perspective.
entities.note:
**Span name** SHOULD be set to {process.executable.name}.
Instrumentations that have additional context about executed commands MAY use
a different low-cardinality span name format and SHOULD document it.

**Span status** SHOULD follow the [Recording Errors](/docs/general/recording-errors.md) document.
An Error is defined as when the `{process.exit.code}` attribute is not 0.

entities.name: cli.internal
entities.brief: This span describes CLI (Command Line Interfaces) program execution from a callee perspective.
entities.note:
**Span name** SHOULD be set to {process.executable.name}.
Instrumentations that have additional context about executed commands MAY use
a different low-cardinality span name format and SHOULD document it.

**Span status** SHOULD follow the [Recording Errors](/docs/general/recording-errors.md) document.
An Error is defined as when the `{process.exit.code}` attribute is not 0.

entities.name: client.address
entities.brief: Client address - domain name if available without reverse DNS lookup; otherwise, IP address or Unix domain socket name.
entities.note: When observed from the server side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `client.address` SHOULD represent the client address behind any intermediaries,  for example proxies, if it's available.

entities.name: client.port
entities.brief: Client port number.
entities.note: When observed from the server side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `client.port` SHOULD represent the client port behind any intermediaries,  for example proxies, if it's available.

entities.name: cloud
entities.brief: A cloud environment (e.g. GCP, Azure, AWS)

entities.name: cloud.account.id
entities.brief: The cloud account ID the resource is assigned to.
entities.note: For Azure, this is the subscription ID.

entities.name: cloud.availability_zone
entities.brief: Cloud regions often have multiple, isolated locations known as zones to increase availability. Availability zone represents the zone where the resource is running.
entities.note: Availability zones are called "zones" on Alibaba Cloud and Google Cloud.

entities.name: cloud.platform
entities.brief: The cloud platform in use.
entities.note: The prefix of the service SHOULD match the one specified in `cloud.provider`.

entities.name: cloud.provider
entities.brief: Name of the cloud provider.

entities.name: cloud.region
entities.brief: The geographical region within a cloud provider. When associated with a resource, this attribute specifies the region where the resource operates. When calling services or APIs deployed on a cloud, this attribute identifies the region where the called destination is deployed.
entities.note: Refer to your provider's docs to see the available regions, for example [Alibaba Cloud regions](https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/40654.htm), [AWS regions](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/global-infrastructure/regions_az/), [Azure regions](https://azure.microsoft.com/global-infrastructure/geographies/), [Google Cloud regions](https://cloud.google.com/about/locations), or [Tencent Cloud regions](https://www.tencentcloud.com/document/product/213/6091).

entities.name: cloud.resource_id
entities.brief: Cloud provider-specific native identifier of the monitored cloud resource (e.g. an [ARN](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-arns-and-namespaces.html) on AWS, a [fully qualified resource ID](https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/resources/resources/get-by-id) on Azure, a [full resource name](https://google.aip.dev/122#full-resource-names) on GCP)
entities.note:
On some cloud providers, it may not be possible to determine the full ID at startup,
so it may be necessary to set `cloud.resource_id` as a span attribute instead.

The exact value to use for `cloud.resource_id` depends on the cloud provider.
The following well-known definitions MUST be used if you set this attribute and they apply:

- **AWS Lambda:** The function [ARN](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-arns-and-namespaces.html).
  Take care not to use the "invoked ARN" directly but replace any
  [alias suffix](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-aliases.html)
  with the resolved function version, as the same runtime instance may be invocable with
  multiple different aliases.
- **GCP:** The [URI of the resource](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/full-resource-names)
- **Azure:** The [Fully Qualified Resource ID](https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/resources/resources/get-by-id) of the invoked function,
  *not* the function app, having the form
  `/subscriptions/<SUBSCRIPTION_GUID>/resourceGroups/<RG>/providers/Microsoft.Web/sites/<FUNCAPP>/functions/<FUNC>`.
  This means that a span attribute MUST be used, as an Azure function app can host multiple functions that would usually share
  a TracerProvider.

entities.name: cloudevents
entities.brief: This document defines attributes for CloudEvents. CloudEvents is a specification on how to define event data in a standard way. These attributes can be attached to spans when performing operations with CloudEvents, regardless of the protocol being used.

entities.name: cloudevents.event_id
entities.brief: The [event_id](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#id) uniquely identifies the event.

entities.name: cloudevents.event_source
entities.brief: The [source](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#source-1) identifies the context in which an event happened.

entities.name: cloudevents.event_spec_version
entities.brief: The [version of the CloudEvents specification](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#specversion) which the event uses.

entities.name: cloudevents.event_subject
entities.brief: The [subject](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#subject) of the event in the context of the event producer (identified by source).

entities.name: cloudevents.event_type
entities.brief: The [event_type](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#type) contains a value describing the type of event related to the originating occurrence.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.app
entities.brief: The application which is monitored.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.app.id
entities.brief: The guid of the application.
entities.note:
Application instrumentation should use the value from environment
variable `VCAP_APPLICATION.application_id`. This is the same value as
reported by `cf app <app-name> --guid`.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.app.instance.id
entities.brief: The index of the application instance. 0 when just one instance is active.
entities.note:
CloudFoundry defines the `instance_id` in the [Loggregator v2 envelope](https://github.com/cloudfoundry/loggregator-api#v2-envelope).
It is used for logs and metrics emitted by CloudFoundry. It is
supposed to contain the application instance index for applications
deployed on the runtime.

Application instrumentation should use the value from environment
variable `CF_INSTANCE_INDEX`.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.app.name
entities.brief: The name of the application.
entities.note:
Application instrumentation should use the value from environment
variable `VCAP_APPLICATION.application_name`. This is the same value
as reported by `cf apps`.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.org
entities.brief: The organization of the application which is monitored.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.org.id
entities.brief: The guid of the CloudFoundry org the application is running in.
entities.note:
Application instrumentation should use the value from environment
variable `VCAP_APPLICATION.org_id`. This is the same value as
reported by `cf org <org-name> --guid`.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.org.name
entities.brief: The name of the CloudFoundry organization the app is running in.
entities.note:
Application instrumentation should use the value from environment
variable `VCAP_APPLICATION.org_name`. This is the same value as
reported by `cf orgs`.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.process
entities.brief: The process of the application which is monitored.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.process.id
entities.brief: The UID identifying the process.
entities.note:
Application instrumentation should use the value from environment
variable `VCAP_APPLICATION.process_id`. It is supposed to be equal to
`VCAP_APPLICATION.app_id` for applications deployed to the runtime.
For system components, this could be the actual PID.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.process.type
entities.brief: The type of process.
entities.note:
CloudFoundry applications can consist of multiple jobs. Usually the
main process will be of type `web`. There can be additional background
tasks or side-cars with different process types.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.space
entities.brief: The space of the application which is monitored.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.space.id
entities.brief: The guid of the CloudFoundry space the application is running in.
entities.note:
Application instrumentation should use the value from environment
variable `VCAP_APPLICATION.space_id`. This is the same value as
reported by `cf space <space-name> --guid`.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.space.name
entities.brief: The name of the CloudFoundry space the application is running in.
entities.note:
Application instrumentation should use the value from environment
variable `VCAP_APPLICATION.space_name`. This is the same value as
reported by `cf spaces`.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.system
entities.brief: The system component which is monitored.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.system.id
entities.brief: A guid or another name describing the event source.
entities.note:
CloudFoundry defines the `source_id` in the [Loggregator v2 envelope](https://github.com/cloudfoundry/loggregator-api#v2-envelope).
It is used for logs and metrics emitted by CloudFoundry. It is
supposed to contain the component name, e.g. "gorouter", for
CloudFoundry components.

When system components are instrumented, values from the
[Bosh spec](https://bosh.io/docs/jobs/#properties-spec)
should be used. The `system.id` should be set to
`spec.deployment/spec.name`.

entities.name: cloudfoundry.system.instance.id
entities.brief: A guid describing the concrete instance of the event source.
entities.note:
CloudFoundry defines the `instance_id` in the [Loggregator v2 envelope](https://github.com/cloudfoundry/loggregator-api#v2-envelope).
It is used for logs and metrics emitted by CloudFoundry. It is
supposed to contain the vm id for CloudFoundry components.

When system components are instrumented, values from the
[Bosh spec](https://bosh.io/docs/jobs/#properties-spec)
should be used. The `system.instance.id` should be set to `spec.id`.

entities.name: code
entities.brief: These attributes allow to report this unit of code and therefore to provide more context about the span.

entities.name: code.column
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `code.column.number`

entities.name: code.column.number
entities.brief: The column number in `code.file.path` best representing the operation. It SHOULD point within the code unit named in `code.function.name`. This attribute MUST NOT be used on the Profile signal since the data is already captured in 'message Line'. This constraint is imposed to prevent redundancy and maintain data integrity.

entities.name: code.file.path
entities.brief: The source code file name that identifies the code unit as uniquely as possible (preferably an absolute file path). This attribute MUST NOT be used on the Profile signal since the data is already captured in 'message Function'. This constraint is imposed to prevent redundancy and maintain data integrity.

entities.name: code.filepath
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `code.file.path` instead

entities.name: code.function
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `code.function.name` instead

deprecation.note: Value should be included in `code.function.name` which is expected to be a fully-qualified name.

entities.name: code.function.name
entities.brief: The method or function fully-qualified name without arguments. The value should fit the natural representation of the language runtime, which is also likely the same used within `code.stacktrace` attribute value. This attribute MUST NOT be used on the Profile signal since the data is already captured in 'message Function'. This constraint is imposed to prevent redundancy and maintain data integrity.
entities.note:
Values and format depends on each language runtime, thus it is impossible to provide an exhaustive list of examples.
The values are usually the same (or prefixes of) the ones found in native stack trace representation stored in
`code.stacktrace` without information on arguments.

Examples:

* Java method: `com.example.MyHttpService.serveRequest`
* Java anonymous class method: `com.mycompany.Main$1.myMethod`
* Java lambda method: `com.mycompany.Main$$Lambda/0x0000748ae4149c00.myMethod`
* PHP function: `GuzzleHttp\Client::transfer`
* Go function: `github.com/my/repo/pkg.foo.func5`
* Elixir: `OpenTelemetry.Ctx.new`
* Erlang: `opentelemetry_ctx:new`
* Rust: `playground::my_module::my_cool_func`
* C function: `fopen`

entities.name: code.line.number
entities.brief: The line number in `code.file.path` best representing the operation. It SHOULD point within the code unit named in `code.function.name`. This attribute MUST NOT be used on the Profile signal since the data is already captured in 'message Line'. This constraint is imposed to prevent redundancy and maintain data integrity.

entities.name: code.lineno
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `code.line.number` instead

entities.name: code.namespace
entities.brief: Deprecated, namespace is now included into `code.function.name`

deprecation.note: Value should be included in `code.function.name` which is expected to be a fully-qualified name.

entities.name: code.stacktrace
entities.brief: A stacktrace as a string in the natural representation for the language runtime. The representation is identical to [`exception.stacktrace`](/docs/exceptions/exceptions-spans.md#stacktrace-representation). This attribute MUST NOT be used on the Profile signal since the data is already captured in 'message Location'. This constraint is imposed to prevent redundancy and maintain data integrity.

entities.name: container
entities.brief: A container instance.

entities.name: container.command
entities.brief: The command used to run the container (i.e. the command name).
entities.note: If using embedded credentials or sensitive data, it is recommended to remove them to prevent potential leakage.

entities.name: container.command_args
entities.brief: All the command arguments (including the command/executable itself) run by the container.

entities.name: container.command_line
entities.brief: The full command run by the container as a single string representing the full command.

entities.name: container.cpu.state
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `cpu.mode` instead.

entities.name: container.cpu.time
entities.brief: CPU time consumed.
entities.note: CPU time consumed by the specific container on all available CPU cores

entities.name: container.cpu.usage
entities.brief: Container's CPU usage, measured in cpus. Range from 0 to the number of allocatable CPUs.
entities.note: CPU usage of the specific container on all available CPU cores, averaged over the sample window

entities.name: container.csi.plugin.name
entities.brief: The name of the CSI ([Container Storage Interface](https://github.com/container-storage-interface/spec)) plugin used by the volume.
entities.note: This can sometimes be referred to as a "driver" in CSI implementations. This should represent the `name` field of the GetPluginInfo RPC.

entities.name: container.csi.volume.id
entities.brief: The unique volume ID returned by the CSI ([Container Storage Interface](https://github.com/container-storage-interface/spec)) plugin.
entities.note: This can sometimes be referred to as a "volume handle" in CSI implementations. This should represent the `Volume.volume_id` field in CSI spec.

entities.name: container.disk.io
entities.brief: Disk bytes for the container.
entities.note: The total number of bytes read/written successfully (aggregated from all disks).

entities.name: container.filesystem.available
entities.brief: Container filesystem available bytes.
entities.note:
In K8s, this metric is derived from the
[FsStats.AvailableBytes](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.33.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#FsStats) field
of the [ContainerStats.Rootfs](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.33.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#ContainerStats)
of the Kubelet's stats API.

entities.name: container.filesystem.capacity
entities.brief: Container filesystem capacity.
entities.note:
In K8s, this metric is derived from the
[FsStats.CapacityBytes](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.33.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#FsStats) field
of the [ContainerStats.Rootfs](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.33.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#ContainerStats)
of the Kubelet's stats API.

entities.name: container.filesystem.usage
entities.brief: Container filesystem usage.
entities.note:
This may not equal capacity - available.

In K8s, this metric is derived from the
[FsStats.UsedBytes](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.33.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#FsStats) field
of the [ContainerStats.Rootfs](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.33.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#ContainerStats)
of the Kubelet's stats API.

entities.name: container.id
entities.brief: Container ID. Usually a UUID, as for example used to [identify Docker containers](https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/run/#container-identification). The UUID might be abbreviated.

entities.name: container.image
entities.brief: The image used for the container.

entities.name: container.image.id
entities.brief: Runtime specific image identifier. Usually a hash algorithm followed by a UUID.
entities.note:
Docker defines a sha256 of the image id; `container.image.id` corresponds to the `Image` field from the Docker container inspect [API](https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/version/v1.52/#tag/Container/operation/ContainerInspect) endpoint.
K8s defines a link to the container registry repository with digest `"imageID": "registry.azurecr.io /namespace/service/dockerfile@sha256:bdeabd40c3a8a492eaf9e8e44d0ebbb84bac7ee25ac0cf8a7159d25f62555625"`.
The ID is assigned by the container runtime and can vary in different environments. Consider using `oci.manifest.digest` if it is important to identify the same image in different environments/runtimes.

entities.name: container.image.name
entities.brief: Name of the image the container was built on.

entities.name: container.image.repo_digests
entities.brief: Repo digests of the container image as provided by the container runtime.
entities.note: [Docker](https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/version/v1.52/#tag/Image/operation/ImageInspect) and [CRI](https://github.com/kubernetes/cri-api/blob/c75ef5b473bbe2d0a4fc92f82235efd665ea8e9f/pkg/apis/runtime/v1/api.proto#L1237-L1238) report those under the `RepoDigests` field.

entities.name: container.image.tags
entities.brief: Container image tags. An example can be found in [Docker Image Inspect](https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/version/v1.52/#tag/Image/operation/ImageInspect). Should be only the `<tag>` section of the full name for example from `registry.example.com/my-org/my-image:<tag>`.

entities.name: container.label
entities.brief: Container labels, `<key>` being the label name, the value being the label value.
entities.note: For example, a docker container label `app` with value `nginx` SHOULD be recorded as the `container.label.app` attribute with value `"nginx"`.

entities.name: container.labels
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `container.label` instead.

entities.name: container.memory.available
entities.brief: Container memory available.
entities.note:
Available memory for use.  This is defined as the memory limit - workingSetBytes. If memory limit is undefined, the available bytes is omitted.
In general, this metric can be derived from [cadvisor](https://github.com/google/cadvisor/blob/v0.53.0/docs/storage/prometheus.md#prometheus-container-metrics) and by subtracting the `container_memory_working_set_bytes` metric from the `container_spec_memory_limit_bytes` metric.
In K8s, this metric is derived from the [MemoryStats.AvailableBytes](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.34.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#MemoryStats) field of the [PodStats.Memory](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.34.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#PodStats) of the Kubelet's stats API.

entities.name: container.memory.paging.faults
entities.brief: Container memory paging faults.
entities.note:
In general, this metric can be derived from [cadvisor](https://github.com/google/cadvisor/blob/v0.53.0/docs/storage/prometheus.md#prometheus-container-metrics) and specifically the `container_memory_failures_total{failure_type=pgfault, scope=container}` and `container_memory_failures_total{failure_type=pgmajfault, scope=container}`metric.
In K8s, this metric is derived from the [MemoryStats.PageFaults](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.34.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#MemoryStats) and [MemoryStats.MajorPageFaults](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.34.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#MemoryStats) field of the [PodStats.Memory](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.34.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#PodStats) of the Kubelet's stats API.

entities.name: container.memory.rss
entities.brief: Container memory RSS.
entities.note:
In general, this metric can be derived from [cadvisor](https://github.com/google/cadvisor/blob/v0.53.0/docs/storage/prometheus.md#prometheus-container-metrics) and specifically the `container_memory_rss` metric.
In K8s, this metric is derived from the [MemoryStats.RSSBytes](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.34.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#MemoryStats) field of the [PodStats.Memory](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.34.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#PodStats) of the Kubelet's stats API.

entities.name: container.memory.usage
entities.brief: Memory usage of the container.
entities.note: Memory usage of the container.

entities.name: container.memory.working_set
entities.brief: Container memory working set.
entities.note:
In general, this metric can be derived from [cadvisor](https://github.com/google/cadvisor/blob/v0.53.0/docs/storage/prometheus.md#prometheus-container-metrics) and specifically the `container_memory_working_set_bytes` metric.
In K8s, this metric is derived from the [MemoryStats.WorkingSetBytes](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.34.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#MemoryStats) field of the [PodStats.Memory](https://pkg.go.dev/k8s.io/kubelet@v0.34.0/pkg/apis/stats/v1alpha1#PodStats) of the Kubelet's stats API.

entities.name: container.name
entities.brief: Container name used by container runtime.

entities.name: container.network.io
entities.brief: Network bytes for the container.
entities.note: The number of bytes sent/received on all network interfaces by the container.

entities.name: container.runtime
entities.brief: The container runtime managing this container.

entities.name: container.runtime
entities.brief: The runtime being used to run the container

entities.name: container.runtime.description
entities.brief: A description about the runtime which could include, for example details about the CRI/API version being used or other customizations.

entities.name: container.runtime.name
entities.brief: The container runtime managing this container.

entities.name: container.runtime.version
entities.brief: The version of the runtime of this process, as returned by the runtime without modification.

entities.name: container.uptime
entities.brief: The time the container has been running.
entities.note:
Instrumentations SHOULD use a gauge with type `double` and measure uptime in seconds as a floating point number with the highest precision available.
The actual accuracy would depend on the instrumentation and operating system.

entities.name: cpu.frequency
entities.brief: Deprecated. Use `system.cpu.frequency` instead.

entities.name: cpu.logical_number
entities.brief: The logical CPU number [0..n-1]

entities.name: cpu.mode
entities.brief: The mode of the CPU

entities.name: cpu.time
entities.brief: Deprecated. Use `system.cpu.time` instead.

entities.name: cpu.utilization
entities.brief: Deprecated. Use `system.cpu.utilization` instead.

entities.name: cpython.gc.collected_objects
entities.brief: The total number of objects collected inside a generation since interpreter start.
entities.note: This metric reports data from [`gc.stats()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/gc.html#gc.get_stats).

entities.name: cpython.gc.collections
entities.brief: The number of times a generation was collected since interpreter start.
entities.note: This metric reports data from [`gc.stats()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/gc.html#gc.get_stats).

entities.name: cpython.gc.generation
entities.brief: Value of the garbage collector collection generation.

entities.name: cpython.gc.uncollectable_objects
entities.brief: The total number of objects which were found to be uncollectable inside a generation since interpreter start.
entities.note: This metric reports data from [`gc.stats()`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/gc.html#gc.get_stats).

entities.name: db
entities.brief: This span defines the attributes used to perform database client calls.

entities.name: db.cassandra
entities.brief: Attributes for Cassandra

entities.name: db.cassandra.client
entities.brief: Spans representing calls to a Cassandra database adhere to the general [Semantic Conventions for Database Client Spans](/docs/db/database-spans.md).
entities.note:
`db.system.name` MUST be set to `"cassandra"` and SHOULD be provided **at span creation time**.

**Span name** SHOULD follow the general [database span name convention](/docs/db/database-spans.md#name)

entities.name: db.cassandra.consistency_level
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `cassandra.consistency.level` instead.

entities.name: db.cassandra.coordinator.dc
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `cassandra.coordinator.dc` instead.

entities.name: db.cassandra.coordinator.id
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `cassandra.coordinator.id` instead.

entities.name: db.cassandra.idempotence
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `cassandra.query.idempotent` instead.

entities.name: db.cassandra.page_size
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `cassandra.page.size` instead.

entities.name: db.cassandra.speculative_execution_count
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `cassandra.speculative_execution.count` instead.

entities.name: db.cassandra.table
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.collection.name` instead.

entities.name: db.client
entities.brief: This span describes database client call.
entities.note:
Instrumentations SHOULD, when possible, record database spans that represent the logical database
operation as observed by the caller (such as client application).

When a database client provides higher-level convenience APIs for specific operations
(e.g., calling a stored procedure), which internally generate and execute a generic query,
it is RECOMMENDED to instrument the higher-level convenience APIs.
These often allow setting `db.operation.*` attributes, which usually are not
readily available at the generic query level.

**Span name** is covered in the [Name](/docs/db/database-spans.md#name) section.

**Span duration** is covered in the [Database client span duration](/docs/db/database-spans.md#database-client-span-duration) section.

**Span kind** SHOULD be `CLIENT`. It MAY be set to `INTERNAL` on spans representing
in-memory database calls.
It's RECOMMENDED to use `CLIENT` kind when database system being instrumented usually
runs in a different process than its client or when database calls happen over
instrumented protocol such as HTTP.

**Span status** SHOULD follow the [Recording Errors](/docs/general/recording-errors.md) document.
Semantic conventions for individual systems SHOULD specify which values of `db.response.status_code`
classify as errors.

entities.name: db.client.connection.count
entities.brief: The number of connections that are currently in state described by the `state` attribute.

entities.name: db.client.connection.create_time
entities.brief: The time it took to create a new connection.

entities.name: db.client.connection.idle.max
entities.brief: The maximum number of idle open connections allowed.

entities.name: db.client.connection.idle.min
entities.brief: The minimum number of idle open connections allowed.

entities.name: db.client.connection.max
entities.brief: The maximum number of open connections allowed.

entities.name: db.client.connection.pending_requests
entities.brief: The number of current pending requests for an open connection.

entities.name: db.client.connection.pool.name
entities.brief: The name of the connection pool; unique within the instrumented application. In case the connection pool implementation doesn't provide a name, instrumentation SHOULD use a combination of parameters that would make the name unique, for example, combining attributes `server.address`, `server.port`, and `db.namespace`, formatted as `server.address:server.port/db.namespace`. Instrumentations that generate connection pool name following different patterns SHOULD document it.

entities.name: db.client.connection.state
entities.brief: The state of a connection in the pool

entities.name: db.client.connection.timeouts
entities.brief: The number of connection timeouts that have occurred trying to obtain a connection from the pool.

entities.name: db.client.connection.use_time
entities.brief: The time between borrowing a connection and returning it to the pool.

entities.name: db.client.connection.wait_time
entities.brief: The time it took to obtain an open connection from the pool.

entities.name: db.client.connections.create_time
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.client.connection.create_time` instead. Note: the unit also changed from `ms` to `s`.

entities.name: db.client.connections.idle.max
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.client.connection.idle.max` instead.

entities.name: db.client.connections.idle.min
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.client.connection.idle.min` instead.

entities.name: db.client.connections.max
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.client.connection.max` instead.

entities.name: db.client.connections.pending_requests
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.client.connection.pending_requests` instead.

entities.name: db.client.connections.pool.name
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.client.connection.pool.name` instead.

entities.name: db.client.connections.state
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.client.connection.state` instead.

entities.name: db.client.connections.timeouts
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.client.connection.timeouts` instead.

entities.name: db.client.connections.usage
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.client.connection.count` instead.

entities.name: db.client.connections.use_time
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.client.connection.use_time` instead. Note: the unit also changed from `ms` to `s`.

entities.name: db.client.connections.wait_time
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.client.connection.wait_time` instead. Note: the unit also changed from `ms` to `s`.

entities.name: db.client.cosmosdb.active_instance.count
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `azure.cosmosdb.client.active_instance.count` instead.

entities.name: db.client.cosmosdb.operation.request_charge
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `azure.cosmosdb.client.operation.request_charge` instead.

entities.name: db.client.operation.duration
entities.brief: Duration of database client operations.
entities.note: Batch operations SHOULD be recorded as a single operation.

entities.name: db.client.operation.exception
entities.brief: This event represents an exception that occurred during a database client operation, such as connection failures, query errors, timeouts, or other errors that prevent the operation from completing successfully.
entities.note:
This event SHOULD be recorded when an exception occurs during database client operations.
Instrumentations SHOULD set the severity to WARN (severity number 13) when recording this event.
Instrumentations MAY provide a configuration option to populate exception events with the attributes captured on the corresponding database client span.

entities.name: db.client.response.returned_rows
entities.brief: The actual number of records returned by the database operation.

entities.name: db.collection.name
entities.brief: The name of a collection (table, container) within the database.
entities.note:
It is RECOMMENDED to capture the value as provided by the application
without attempting to do any case normalization.

The collection name SHOULD NOT be extracted from `db.query.text`,
when the database system supports query text with multiple collections
in non-batch operations.

For batch operations, if the individual operations are known to have the same
collection name then that collection name SHOULD be used.

entities.name: db.connection_string
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `server.address`, `server.port` attributes instead.

entities.name: db.cosmosdb
entities.brief: Attributes for Cosmos DB.

entities.name: db.cosmosdb.client
entities.brief: Attributes for Cosmos DB.

entities.name: db.cosmosdb.client_id
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `azure.client.id` instead.

entities.name: db.cosmosdb.connection_mode
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `azure.cosmosdb.connection.mode` instead.

entities.name: db.cosmosdb.consistency_level
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `cosmosdb.consistency.level` instead.

entities.name: db.cosmosdb.container
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.collection.name` instead.

entities.name: db.cosmosdb.operation_type
entities.brief: Deprecated, no replacement at this time.

deprecation.note: Removed, no replacement at this time.

entities.name: db.cosmosdb.regions_contacted
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `azure.cosmosdb.operation.contacted_regions` instead.

entities.name: db.cosmosdb.request_charge
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `azure.cosmosdb.operation.request_charge` instead.

entities.name: db.cosmosdb.request_content_length
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `azure.cosmosdb.request.body.size` instead.

entities.name: db.cosmosdb.status_code
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.response.status_code` instead.

entities.name: db.cosmosdb.sub_status_code
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `azure.cosmosdb.response.sub_status_code` instead.

entities.name: db.couchdb
entities.brief: Attributes for CouchDB

entities.name: db.couchdb.client
entities.brief: Spans representing calls to CouchDB adhere to the general [Semantic Conventions for Database Client Spans](/docs/db/database-spans.md).
entities.note:
`db.system.name` MUST be set to `"couchdb"` and SHOULD be provided **at span creation time**.

**Span name** SHOULD follow the general [database span name convention](/docs/db/database-spans.md#name)

entities.name: db.elasticsearch
entities.brief: Attributes for Elasticsearch

entities.name: db.elasticsearch.client
entities.brief: Spans representing calls to Elasticsearch adhere to the general [Semantic Conventions for Database Client Spans](/docs/db/database-spans.md).
entities.note:
`db.system.name` MUST be set to `"elasticsearch"` and SHOULD be provided **at span creation time**.

**Span name** SHOULD follow the general [database span name convention](/docs/db/database-spans.md#name)
with the endpoint identifier stored in `db.operation.name`, and the index stored in `db.collection.name`.

entities.name: db.elasticsearch.cluster.name
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.namespace` instead.

entities.name: db.elasticsearch.node.name
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `elasticsearch.node.name` instead.

entities.name: db.elasticsearch.path_parts
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.operation.parameter` instead.

entities.name: db.hbase
entities.brief: Attributes for HBase

entities.name: db.hbase.client
entities.brief: Spans representing calls to an HBase database adhere to the general [Semantic Conventions for Database Client Spans](/docs/db/database-spans.md).
entities.note:
`db.system.name` MUST be set to `"hbase"` and SHOULD be provided **at span creation time**.

**Span name** SHOULD follow the general [database span name convention](/docs/db/database-spans.md#name)

entities.name: db.instance.id
entities.brief: Deprecated, no general replacement at this time. For Elasticsearch, use `db.elasticsearch.node.name` instead.

deprecation.note: Removed, no general replacement at this time. For Elasticsearch, use `db.elasticsearch.node.name` instead.

entities.name: db.jdbc.driver_classname
entities.brief: Removed, no replacement at this time.

deprecation.note: Removed, no replacement at this time.

entities.name: db.mariadb
entities.brief: Attributes for MariaDB

entities.name: db.mariadb.client
entities.brief: Spans representing calls to MariaDB adhere to the general [Semantic Conventions for Database Client Spans](/docs/db/README.md).
entities.note: `db.system.name` MUST be set to `"mariadb"` and SHOULD be provided **at span creation time**.

entities.name: db.mongodb
entities.brief: Attributes for MongoDB

entities.name: db.mongodb.client
entities.brief: Spans representing calls to MongoDB adhere to the general [Semantic Conventions for Database Client Spans](/docs/db/database-spans.md).
entities.note:
`db.system.name` MUST be set to `"mongodb"` and SHOULD be provided **at span creation time**.
**Span name** SHOULD follow the general [database span name convention](/docs/db/database-spans.md#name)

entities.name: db.mongodb.collection
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.collection.name` instead.

entities.name: db.mssql
entities.brief: Attributes for Microsoft SQL Server

entities.name: db.mssql.client
entities.brief: Attributes for Microsoft SQL Server

entities.name: db.mssql.instance_name
entities.brief: Deprecated, SQL Server instance is now populated as a part of `db.namespace` attribute.

deprecation.note: Removed, no replacement at this time.

entities.name: db.mysql
entities.brief: Attributes for MySQL

entities.name: db.mysql.client
entities.brief: Spans representing calls to a MySQL Server adhere to the general [Semantic Conventions for Database Client Spans](database-spans.md).
entities.note: `db.system.name` MUST be set to `"mysql"` and SHOULD be provided **at span creation time**.

entities.name: db.name
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.namespace` instead.

entities.name: db.namespace
entities.brief: The name of the database, fully qualified within the server address and port.
entities.note:
If a database system has multiple namespace components, they SHOULD be concatenated from the most general to the most specific namespace component, using `|` as a separator between the components. Any missing components (and their associated separators) SHOULD be omitted.
Semantic conventions for individual database systems SHOULD document what `db.namespace` means in the context of that system.
It is RECOMMENDED to capture the value as provided by the application without attempting to do any case normalization.

entities.name: db.operation
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.operation.name` instead.

entities.name: db.operation.batch.size
entities.brief: The number of database operations included in a batch operation.
entities.note:
Except for empty batch requests described below, a batch operation contains two
or more database operations explicitly submitted as separate operations in a single
client call, protocol message, or database command.

Requests to batch APIs that contain only one operation SHOULD be modeled as single
operations, not as batch operations.

A database call is not a batch operation solely because one operation accepts
multiple operands, such as keys, rows, documents, points, or other data elements,
including Redis [`MGET`](https://redis.io/docs/latest/commands/mget/) with
multiple keys.

In batch APIs that execute the same parameterized operation with parameter sets,
each parameter set represents one database operation for determining whether the
request is a batch operation. Requests with only one parameter set SHOULD be modeled
