entities.name: openshift.clusterquota.uid
entities.brief: The UID of the cluster quota.

entities.name: opentracing
entities.brief: This document defines semantic conventions for the OpenTracing Shim
entities.note: These conventions are used by the OpenTracing Shim layer.

entities.name: opentracing.ref_type
entities.brief: Parent-child Reference type
entities.note: The causal relationship between a child Span and a parent Span.

entities.name: oracle_cloud.realm
entities.brief: The OCI realm identifier that indicates the isolated partition in which the tenancy and its resources reside.
entities.note: See [OCI documentation on realms](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/General/Concepts/regions.htm)

entities.name: oracle.db.domain
entities.brief: The database domain associated with the connection.
entities.note:
This attribute SHOULD be set to the value of the `DB_DOMAIN` initialization parameter,
as exposed in `v$parameter`. `DB_DOMAIN` defines the domain portion of the global
database name and SHOULD be configured when a database is, or may become, part of a
distributed environment. Its value consists of one or more valid identifiers
(alphanumeric ASCII characters) separated by periods.

entities.name: oracle.db.instance.name
entities.brief: The instance name associated with the connection in an Oracle Real Application Clusters environment.
entities.note:
There can be multiple instances associated with a single database service. It indicates the
unique instance name to which the connection is currently bound. For non-RAC databases, this value
defaults to the `oracle.db.name`.

entities.name: oracle.db.name
entities.brief: The database name associated with the connection.
entities.note: This attribute SHOULD be set to the value of the parameter `DB_NAME` exposed in `v$parameter`.

entities.name: oracle.db.pdb
entities.brief: The pluggable database (PDB) name associated with the connection.
entities.note:
This attribute SHOULD reflect the PDB that the session is currently connected to.
If instrumentation cannot reliably obtain the active PDB name for each operation
without issuing an additional query (such as `SELECT SYS_CONTEXT`), it is
RECOMMENDED to fall back to the PDB name specified at connection establishment.

entities.name: oracle.db.service
entities.brief: The service name currently associated with the database connection.
entities.note:
The effective service name for a connection can change during its lifetime,
for example after executing sql, `ALTER SESSION`. If an instrumentation cannot reliably
obtain the current service name for each operation without issuing an additional
query (such as `SELECT SYS_CONTEXT`), it is RECOMMENDED to fall back to the
service name originally provided at connection establishment.

entities.name: os
entities.brief: The operating system (OS) on which the process represented by this resource is running.
entities.note: In case of virtualized environments, this is the operating system as it is observed by the process, i.e., the virtualized guest rather than the underlying host.

entities.name: os.build_id
entities.brief: Unique identifier for a particular build or compilation of the operating system.

entities.name: os.description
entities.brief: Human readable (not intended to be parsed) OS version information, like e.g. reported by `ver` or `lsb_release -a` commands.

entities.name: os.name
entities.brief: Human readable operating system name.

entities.name: os.type
entities.brief: The operating system type.

entities.name: os.version
entities.brief: The version string of the operating system as defined in [Version Attributes](/docs/resource/README.md#version-attributes).

entities.name: otel_span
entities.brief: Span attributes used by non-OTLP exporters to represent OpenTelemetry Span's concepts.

entities.name: otel.component.name
entities.brief: A name uniquely identifying the instance of the OpenTelemetry component within its containing SDK instance.
entities.note:
Implementations SHOULD ensure a low cardinality for this attribute, even across application or SDK restarts.
E.g. implementations MUST NOT use UUIDs as values for this attribute.

Implementations MAY achieve these goals by following a `<otel.component.type>/<instance-counter>` pattern, e.g. `batching_span_processor/0`.
Hereby `otel.component.type` refers to the corresponding attribute value of the component.

The value of `instance-counter` MAY be automatically assigned by the component and uniqueness within the enclosing SDK instance MUST be guaranteed.
For example, `<instance-counter>` MAY be implemented by using a monotonically increasing counter (starting with `0`), which is incremented every time an
instance of the given component type is started.

With this implementation, for example the first Batching Span Processor would have `batching_span_processor/0`
as `otel.component.name`, the second one `batching_span_processor/1` and so on.
These values will therefore be reused in the case of an application restart.

entities.name: otel.component.type
entities.brief: A name identifying the type of the OpenTelemetry component.
entities.note:
If none of the standardized values apply, implementations SHOULD use the language-defined name of the type.
E.g. for Java the fully qualified classname SHOULD be used in this case.

entities.name: otel.event.name
entities.brief: Identifies the class / type of event.
entities.note: This attribute SHOULD be used by non-OTLP exporters when destination does not support `EventName` or equivalent field. This attribute MAY be used by applications using existing logging libraries so that it can be used to set the `EventName` field by Collector or SDK components.

entities.name: otel.library.name
entities.brief: Deprecated. Use the `otel.scope.name` attribute

entities.name: otel.library.version
entities.brief: Deprecated. Use the `otel.scope.version` attribute.

entities.name: otel.scope
entities.brief: Attributes used by non-OTLP exporters to represent OpenTelemetry Scope's concepts.

entities.name: otel.scope.name
entities.brief: The name of the instrumentation scope - (`InstrumentationScope.Name` in OTLP).

entities.name: otel.scope.schema_url
entities.brief: The schema URL of the instrumentation scope.

entities.name: otel.scope.version
entities.brief: The version of the instrumentation scope - (`InstrumentationScope.Version` in OTLP).

entities.name: otel.sdk.exporter.log.exported
entities.brief: The number of log records for which the export has finished, either successful or failed.
entities.note:
For successful exports, `error.type` MUST NOT be set. For failed exports, `error.type` MUST contain the failure cause.
For exporters with partial success semantics (e.g. OTLP with `rejected_log_records`), rejected log records MUST count as failed and only non-rejected log records count as success.
If no rejection reason is available, `rejected` SHOULD be used as value for `error.type`.
If the exporter retries failed export attempts, the export operation is considered finished only after the final attempt has concluded.
Each log record MUST be counted exactly once per export operation: intermediate failed attempts that are followed by a retry MUST NOT increment the counter,
and `error.type` reflects the cause of the final attempt.

entities.name: otel.sdk.exporter.log.inflight
entities.brief: The number of log records which were passed to the exporter, but that have not been exported yet (neither successful, nor failed).
entities.note:
Log records are counted as inflight from when they are passed to the exporter until the export operation has concluded.
If the exporter retries failed export attempts, log records remain inflight across all retry attempts and any backoff between them.

entities.name: otel.sdk.exporter.metric_data_point.exported
entities.brief: The number of metric data points for which the export has finished, either successful or failed.
entities.note:
For successful exports, `error.type` MUST NOT be set. For failed exports, `error.type` MUST contain the failure cause.
For exporters with partial success semantics (e.g. OTLP with `rejected_data_points`), rejected data points MUST count as failed and only non-rejected data points count as success.
If no rejection reason is available, `rejected` SHOULD be used as value for `error.type`.
If the exporter retries failed export attempts, the export operation is considered finished only after the final attempt has concluded.
Each metric data point MUST be counted exactly once per export operation: intermediate failed attempts that are followed by a retry MUST NOT increment the counter,
and `error.type` reflects the cause of the final attempt.

entities.name: otel.sdk.exporter.metric_data_point.inflight
entities.brief: The number of metric data points which were passed to the exporter, but that have not been exported yet (neither successful, nor failed).
entities.note:
Metric data points are counted as inflight from when they are passed to the exporter until the export operation has concluded.
If the exporter retries failed export attempts, metric data points remain inflight across all retry attempts and any backoff between them.

entities.name: otel.sdk.exporter.operation.duration
entities.brief: The duration of exporting a batch of telemetry records.
entities.note:
This metric defines successful operations using the full success definitions for [http](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-proto/blob/v1.5.0/docs/specification.md#full-success-1)
and [grpc](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-proto/blob/v1.5.0/docs/specification.md#full-success). Anything else is defined as an unsuccessful operation. For successful
operations, `error.type` MUST NOT be set. For unsuccessful export operations, `error.type` MUST contain a relevant failure cause.
If the exporter retries failed export attempts, exactly one observation MUST be recorded per export operation,
covering the wall-clock duration from the start of the first attempt through the conclusion of the final attempt (including any backoff between attempts).
`error.type` reflects the cause of the final attempt.

entities.name: otel.sdk.exporter.span.exported
entities.brief: The number of spans for which the export has finished, either successful or failed.
entities.note:
For successful exports, `error.type` MUST NOT be set. For failed exports, `error.type` MUST contain the failure cause.
For exporters with partial success semantics (e.g. OTLP with `rejected_spans`), rejected spans MUST count as failed and only non-rejected spans count as success.
If no rejection reason is available, `rejected` SHOULD be used as value for `error.type`.
If the exporter retries failed export attempts, the export operation is considered finished only after the final attempt has concluded.
Each span MUST be counted exactly once per export operation: intermediate failed attempts that are followed by a retry MUST NOT increment the counter,
and `error.type` reflects the cause of the final attempt.

entities.name: otel.sdk.exporter.span.exported.count
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `otel.sdk.exporter.span.exported` instead.

entities.name: otel.sdk.exporter.span.inflight
entities.brief: The number of spans which were passed to the exporter, but that have not been exported yet (neither successful, nor failed).
entities.note:
Spans are counted as inflight from when they are passed to the exporter until the export operation has concluded.
If the exporter retries failed export attempts, spans remain inflight across all retry attempts and any backoff between them.

entities.name: otel.sdk.exporter.span.inflight.count
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `otel.sdk.exporter.span.inflight` instead.

entities.name: otel.sdk.log.created
entities.brief: The number of logs submitted to enabled SDK Loggers.

entities.name: otel.sdk.metric_reader.collection.duration
entities.brief: The duration of the collect operation of the metric reader.
entities.note:
For successful collections, `error.type` MUST NOT be set. For failed collections, `error.type` SHOULD contain the failure cause.
It can happen that metrics collection is successful for some MetricProducers, while others fail. In that case `error.type` SHOULD be set to any of the failure causes.

entities.name: otel.sdk.processor.log.processed
entities.brief: The number of log records for which the processing has finished, either successful or failed.
entities.note:
For successful processing, `error.type` MUST NOT be set. For failed processing, `error.type` MUST contain the failure cause.
SDK Batching Log Record Processors MUST use `queue_full` as the value of `error.type` for log records dropped due to a full queue.
SDK Log Record Processors MUST use `already_shutdown` as the value of `error.type` for log records dropped because the processor has already been shut down.
For the SDK Simple and Batching Log Record Processor a log record is considered to be processed already when it has been submitted to the exporter,
not when the corresponding export call has finished.

entities.name: otel.sdk.processor.log.queue.capacity
entities.brief: The maximum number of log records the queue of a given instance of an SDK Log Record processor can hold.
entities.note: Only applies to Log Record processors which use a queue, e.g. the SDK Batching Log Record Processor.

entities.name: otel.sdk.processor.log.queue.size
entities.brief: The number of log records in the queue of a given instance of an SDK log processor.
entities.note: Only applies to log record processors which use a queue, e.g. the SDK Batching Log Record Processor.

entities.name: otel.sdk.processor.span.processed
entities.brief: The number of spans for which the processing has finished, either successful or failed.
entities.note:
For successful processing, `error.type` MUST NOT be set. For failed processing, `error.type` MUST contain the failure cause.
SDK Batching Span Processors MUST use `queue_full` as the value of `error.type` for spans dropped due to a full queue.
SDK Span Processors MUST use `already_shutdown` as the value of `error.type` for spans dropped because the processor has already been shut down.
For the SDK Simple and Batching Span Processor a span is considered to be processed already when it has been submitted to the exporter, not when the corresponding export call has finished.

entities.name: otel.sdk.processor.span.processed.count
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `otel.sdk.processor.span.processed` instead.

entities.name: otel.sdk.processor.span.queue.capacity
entities.brief: The maximum number of spans the queue of a given instance of an SDK span processor can hold.
entities.note: Only applies to span processors which use a queue, e.g. the SDK Batching Span Processor.

entities.name: otel.sdk.processor.span.queue.size
entities.brief: The number of spans in the queue of a given instance of an SDK span processor.
entities.note: Only applies to span processors which use a queue, e.g. the SDK Batching Span Processor.

entities.name: otel.sdk.span.ended
entities.brief: Use `otel.sdk.span.started` minus `otel.sdk.span.live` to derive this value.

entities.name: otel.sdk.span.ended.count
entities.brief: Use `otel.sdk.span.started` minus `otel.sdk.span.live` to derive this value.

entities.name: otel.sdk.span.live
entities.brief: The number of created spans with `recording=true` for which the end operation has not been called yet.
entities.note: Non-recording spans are not counted, hence `otel.span.sampling_result` can only take values `RECORD_ONLY` and `RECORD_AND_SAMPLE`, not `DROP`.

entities.name: otel.sdk.span.live.count
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `otel.sdk.span.live` instead.

entities.name: otel.sdk.span.started
entities.brief: The number of created spans.
entities.note: Implementations MUST record this metric for all spans, even for non-recording ones.

entities.name: otel.span.parent.origin
entities.brief: Determines whether the span has a parent span, and if so, [whether it is a remote parent](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/trace/api/#isremote)

entities.name: otel.span.sampling_result
entities.brief: The result value of the sampler for this span

entities.name: otel.status_code
entities.brief: Name of the code, either "OK" or "ERROR". MUST NOT be set if the status code is UNSET.

entities.name: otel.status_description
entities.brief: Description of the Status if it has a value, otherwise not set.

entities.name: peer
entities.brief: Operations that access some remote service.

entities.name: peer.service
entities.brief: The [`service.name`](/docs/resource/README.md#service) of the remote service. SHOULD be equal to the actual `service.name` resource attribute of the remote service if any.
entities.note:
Examples of `peer.service` that users may specify:

- A Redis cache of auth tokens as `peer.service="AuthTokenCache"`.
- A gRPC service `rpc.service="io.opentelemetry.AuthService"` may be hosted in both a gateway, `peer.service="ExternalApiService"` and a backend, `peer.service="AuthService"`.

entities.name: pool.name
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `db.client.connection.pool.name` instead.

entities.name: pprof.location.is_folded
entities.brief: Provides an indication that multiple symbols map to this location's address, for example due to identical code folding by the linker. In that case the line information represents one of the multiple symbols. This field must be recomputed when the symbolization state of the profile changes.

entities.name: pprof.mapping.has_filenames
entities.brief: Indicates that there are filenames related to this mapping.

entities.name: pprof.mapping.has_functions
entities.brief: Indicates that there are functions related to this mapping.

entities.name: pprof.mapping.has_inline_frames
entities.brief: Indicates that there are inline frames related to this mapping.

entities.name: pprof.mapping.has_line_numbers
entities.brief: Indicates that there are line numbers related to this mapping.

entities.name: pprof.profile.comment
entities.brief: Free-form text associated with the profile. This field should not be used to store any machine-readable information, it is only for human-friendly content.

entities.name: pprof.profile.doc_url
entities.brief: Documentation link for this profile type.
entities.note: The URL must be absolute and may be missing if the profile was generated by code that did not supply a link

entities.name: pprof.profile.drop_frames
entities.brief: Frames with Function.function_name fully matching the regexp will be dropped from the samples, along with their successors.

entities.name: pprof.profile.keep_frames
entities.brief: Frames with Function.function_name fully matching the regexp will be kept, even if it matches drop_frames.

entities.name: pprof.scope.default_sample_type
entities.brief: Records the pprof's default_sample_type in the original profile. Not set if the default sample type was missing.
entities.note: This attribute, if present, MUST be set at the scope level (resource_profiles[].scope_profiles[].scope.attributes[]).

entities.name: pprof.scope.sample_type_order
entities.brief: Records the indexes of the sample types in the original profile.
entities.note: This attribute, if present, MUST be set at the scope level (resource_profiles[].scope_profiles[].scope.attributes[]).

entities.name: process
entities.brief: An operating system process.

entities.name: process.args_count
entities.brief: Length of the process.command_args array
entities.note: This field can be useful for querying or performing bucket analysis on how many arguments were provided to start a process. More arguments may be an indication of suspicious activity.

entities.name: process.command
entities.brief: The command used to launch the process (i.e. the command name). On Linux based systems, can be set to the zeroth string in `proc/[pid]/cmdline`. On Windows, can be set to the first parameter extracted from `GetCommandLineW`.

entities.name: process.command_args
entities.brief: All the command arguments (including the command/executable itself) as received by the process. On Linux-based systems (and some other Unixoid systems supporting procfs), can be set according to the list of null-delimited strings extracted from `proc/[pid]/cmdline`. For libc-based executables, this would be the full argv vector passed to `main`. SHOULD NOT be collected by default unless there is sanitization that excludes sensitive data.

entities.name: process.command_line
entities.brief: The full command used to launch the process as a single string representing the full command. On Windows, can be set to the result of `GetCommandLineW`. Do not set this if you have to assemble it just for monitoring; use `process.command_args` instead. SHOULD NOT be collected by default unless there is sanitization that excludes sensitive data.

entities.name: process.context_switch_type
entities.brief: "Deprecated, use `process.context_switch.type` instead."

entities.name: process.context_switch.type
entities.brief: Specifies whether the context switches for this data point were voluntary or involuntary.

entities.name: process.context_switches
entities.brief: Number of times the process has been context switched.

entities.name: process.cpu.state
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `cpu.mode` instead.

entities.name: process.cpu.time
entities.brief: Total CPU seconds broken down by different CPU modes.

entities.name: process.cpu.utilization
entities.brief: Difference in process.cpu.time since the last measurement, divided by the elapsed time and number of CPUs available to the process.

entities.name: process.creation.time
entities.brief: The date and time the process was created, in ISO 8601 format.

entities.name: process.disk.io
entities.brief: Disk bytes transferred.

entities.name: process.environment_variable
entities.brief: Process environment variables, `<key>` being the environment variable name, the value being the environment variable value.
entities.note:
Examples:

- an environment variable `USER` with value `"ubuntu"` SHOULD be recorded
as the `process.environment_variable.USER` attribute with value `"ubuntu"`.

- an environment variable `PATH` with value `"/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin"`
SHOULD be recorded as the `process.environment_variable.PATH` attribute
with value `"/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin"`.

entities.name: process.executable
entities.brief: The executable of a process.
entities.note: Represents the executable file associated with a process. A single executable may be run my multiple process instances.

entities.name: process.executable.build_id.gnu
entities.brief: The GNU build ID as found in the `.note.gnu.build-id` ELF section (hex string).

entities.name: process.executable.build_id.go
entities.brief: The Go build ID as retrieved by `go tool buildid <go executable>`.

entities.name: process.executable.build_id.htlhash
entities.brief: Deterministic build ID for executables.
entities.note:
GNU and Go build IDs may be stripped or unavailable in some environments
(e.g., Alpine Linux, Docker images). This attribute provides a deterministic
build ID computed by hashing the first and last 4096 bytes of the file
along with its length:

```
Input   ← Concat(File[:4096], File[-4096:], BigEndianUInt64(Len(File)))
Digest  ← SHA256(Input)
BuildID ← Digest[:16]
```

The result is the first 16 bytes (128 bits) of the SHA256 digest,
represented as a hex string.

entities.name: process.executable.build_id.profiling
entities.brief: "Deprecated, use `process.executable.build_id.htlhash` instead."

entities.name: process.executable.name
entities.brief: The name of the process executable. On Linux based systems, this SHOULD be set to the base name of the target of `/proc/[pid]/exe`. On Windows, this SHOULD be set to the base name of `GetProcessImageFileNameW`.

entities.name: process.executable.path
entities.brief: The full path to the process executable. On Linux based systems, can be set to the target of `proc/[pid]/exe`. On Windows, can be set to the result of `GetProcessImageFileNameW`.

entities.name: process.exit.code
entities.brief: The exit code of the process.

entities.name: process.exit.time
entities.brief: The date and time the process exited, in ISO 8601 format.

entities.name: process.group_leader.pid
entities.brief: The PID of the process's group leader. This is also the process group ID (PGID) of the process.

entities.name: process.interactive
entities.brief: Whether the process is connected to an interactive shell.

entities.name: process.linux.cgroup
entities.brief: The control group associated with the process.
entities.note: Control groups (cgroups) are a kernel feature used to organize and manage process resources. This attribute provides the path(s) to the cgroup(s) associated with the process, which should match the contents of the [/proc/\[PID\]/cgroup](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man7/cgroups.7.html) file.

entities.name: process.memory.usage
entities.brief: The amount of physical memory in use.

entities.name: process.memory.virtual
entities.brief: The amount of committed virtual memory.

entities.name: process.network.io
entities.brief: Network bytes transferred.

entities.name: process.open_file_descriptor.count
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `process.unix.file_descriptor.count` instead.

entities.name: process.owner
entities.brief: The username of the user that owns the process.

entities.name: process.paging.fault_type
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `system.paging.fault.type` instead.

entities.name: process.paging.faults
entities.brief: Number of page faults the process has made.

entities.name: process.parent_pid
entities.brief: Parent Process identifier (PPID).

entities.name: process.pid
entities.brief: Process identifier (PID).

entities.name: process.real_user.id
entities.brief: The real user ID (RUID) of the process.

entities.name: process.real_user.name
entities.brief: The username of the real user of the process.

entities.name: process.runtime
entities.brief: The single (language) runtime instance which is monitored.

entities.name: process.runtime.description
entities.brief: An additional description about the runtime of the process, for example a specific vendor customization of the runtime environment.
entities.examples: Eclipse OpenJ9 Eclipse OpenJ9 VM openj9-0.21.0

entities.name: process.runtime.name
entities.brief: The name of the runtime of this process.

entities.name: process.runtime.version
entities.brief: The version of the runtime of this process, as returned by the runtime without modification.

entities.name: process.saved_user.id
entities.brief: The saved user ID (SUID) of the process.

entities.name: process.saved_user.name
entities.brief: The username of the saved user.

entities.name: process.session_leader.pid
entities.brief: The PID of the process's session leader. This is also the session ID (SID) of the process.

entities.name: process.state
entities.brief: The process state, e.g., [Linux Process State Codes](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man1/ps.1.html#PROCESS_STATE_CODES)

entities.name: process.thread.count
entities.brief: Process threads count.

entities.name: process.title
entities.brief: Process title (proctitle)
entities.note: In many Unix-like systems, process title (proctitle), is the string that represents the name or command line of a running process, displayed by system monitoring tools like ps, top, and htop.

entities.name: process.unix.file_descriptor.count
entities.brief: Number of unix file descriptors in use by the process.

entities.name: process.uptime
entities.brief: The time the process has been running.
entities.note:
Instrumentations SHOULD use a gauge with type `double` and measure uptime in seconds as a floating point number with the highest precision available.
The actual accuracy would depend on the instrumentation and operating system.

entities.name: process.user.id
entities.brief: The effective user ID (EUID) of the process.

entities.name: process.user.name
entities.brief: The username of the effective user of the process.

entities.name: process.vpid
entities.brief: Virtual process identifier.
entities.note: The process ID within a PID namespace. This is not necessarily unique across all processes on the host but it is unique within the process namespace that the process exists within.

entities.name: process.windows.handle.count
entities.brief: Number of handles held by the process.

entities.name: process.working_directory
entities.brief: The working directory of the process.

entities.name: profile.frame.type
entities.brief: Describes the interpreter or compiler of a single frame.

entities.name: rpc
entities.brief: This document defines semantic conventions for remote procedure calls.

entities.name: rpc.call.client
entities.brief: This span represents an outgoing Remote Procedure Call (RPC).
entities.note:
RPC client spans SHOULD cover the entire client-side lifecycle of an RPC,
starting when the RPC is initiated and ending when the response is received
or the RPC is terminated due to an error or cancellation.

For streaming RPCs, the span covers the full lifetime of the request and/or
response streams until they are closed or terminated.

If a transient issue happened and was retried within this RPC, the corresponding
span SHOULD cover the duration of the logical call with all retries.

**Span name:** refer to the [Span Name](/docs/rpc/rpc-spans.md#name) section.

**Span kind** MUST be `CLIENT`.

**Span status**: refer to the [Recording Errors](/docs/general/recording-errors.md)
document for details on how to record span status.

entities.name: rpc.call.server
entities.brief: This span represents an incoming Remote Procedure Call (RPC).
entities.note:
RPC server spans SHOULD cover the entire server-side lifecycle of an RPC,
starting when the request is received and ending when the response is sent
or the RPC is terminated due to an error or cancellation.

For streaming RPCs, the span SHOULD cover the full lifetime of the request
and/or response streams until they are closed or terminated.

**Span name:** refer to the [Span Name](/docs/rpc/rpc-spans.md#name) section.

**Span kind** MUST be `SERVER`.

**Span status**: refer to the [Recording Errors](/docs/general/recording-errors.md)
document for details on how to record span status.

entities.name: rpc.client
entities.brief: This span represents an outgoing Remote Procedure Call (RPC).
entities.note:
**Span name:** refer to the [Span Name](/docs/rpc/rpc-spans.md#span-name) section.

**Span kind** MUST be `CLIENT`.

entities.name: rpc.client.call.duration
entities.brief: Measures the duration of an outgoing Remote Procedure Call (RPC).
entities.note:
When this metric is reported alongside an RPC client span, the metric value
SHOULD be the same as the RPC client span duration.

entities.name: rpc.client.call.exception
entities.brief: This event represents an exception that occurred during an outgoing RPC call, such as network failures, timeouts, serialization errors, or other errors that prevent the call from completing successfully.
entities.note:
This event SHOULD be recorded when an exception occurs during RPC client call operations.
Instrumentations SHOULD set the severity to WARN (severity number 13) when recording this event.
Instrumentations MAY provide a configuration option to populate exception events with the attributes captured on the corresponding RPC client span.

entities.name: rpc.client.duration
entities.brief: Deprecated, use `rpc.client.call.duration` instead. Note: the unit also changed from `ms` to `s`.
entities.note:
While streaming RPCs may record this metric as start-of-batch
to end-of-batch, it's hard to interpret in practice.

**Streaming**: N/A.

entities.name: rpc.client.request.size
entities.brief: Measures the size of RPC request messages (uncompressed).
entities.note: **Streaming**: Recorded per message in a streaming batch

deprecation.note: Removed, no replacement at this time.

entities.name: rpc.client.requests_per_rpc
entities.brief: Measures the number of messages received per RPC.
entities.note:
Should be 1 for all non-streaming RPCs.

**Streaming**: This metric is required for server and client streaming RPCs

deprecation.note: Removed, no replacement at this time.

entities.name: rpc.client.response.size
entities.brief: Measures the size of RPC response messages (uncompressed).
entities.note: **Streaming**: Recorded per response in a streaming batch

deprecation.note: Removed, no replacement at this time.

entities.name: rpc.client.responses_per_rpc
entities.brief: Measures the number of messages sent per RPC.
entities.note:
Should be 1 for all non-streaming RPCs.

**Streaming**: This metric is required for server and client streaming RPCs

deprecation.note: Removed, no replacement at this time.

entities.name: rpc.connect_rpc
entities.brief: Tech-specific attributes for Connect RPC.

entities.name: rpc.connect_rpc.call.client
entities.brief: This span represents an outgoing Remote Procedure Call (RPC).
entities.note:
`rpc.system.name` MUST be set to `"connectrpc"` and SHOULD be provided **at span creation time.**

**Span name:** refer to the [Span Name](/docs/rpc/rpc-spans.md#name) section.

**Span kind** MUST be `CLIENT`.

**Span status**: refer to the [Recording Errors](/docs/general/recording-errors.md)
document for details on how to record span status.

entities.name: rpc.connect_rpc.call.server
entities.brief: This span represents an incoming Remote Procedure Call (RPC).
entities.note:
`rpc.system.name` MUST be set to `"connectrpc"` and SHOULD be provided **at span creation time.**

**Span name:** refer to the [Span Name](/docs/rpc/rpc-spans.md#name) section.

**Span kind** MUST be `SERVER`.

**Span status**: refer to the [Recording Errors](/docs/general/recording-errors.md)
document for details on how to record span status.

entities.name: rpc.jsonrpc.error_code

entities.brief: Deprecated, use the span status description when reporting JSON-RPC spans.

deprecation.note: Use the span status description when reporting JSON-RPC spans.
